item.title: Chile Consumer Prices Jump, Showing Initial Hit From Fuel Hike
item.description: Chile’s consumer prices jumped more than expected last month, reflecting the partial impact of the nation’s biggest hike to fuel costs since at least 1980.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Debuts Bitcoin ETF as Price Slump Rattles Holders
item.description: Morgan Stanley is set to become the first Wall Street bank to launch its own Bitcoin-tracking exchange-traded fund, a milestone for the digital-asset ecosystem that underscores how deeply the original cryptocurrency has embedded itself in the financial mainstream — even as the market around it has cooled.

item.title: Qatar Begins Work to Resume LNG Production After Ceasefire
item.description: Qatar is mobilizing engineers and workers with the aim of resuming production at the world’s biggest liquefied natural gas export plant following a ceasefire in the war in the Middle East, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Galp Curbs Diesel Exports to Shore Up Portugal Fuel Supply
item.description: Galp Energia SGPS SA is limiting diesel exports to build up domestic fuel inventories, as global market volatility drives higher prices and tightens supplies across key trading regions.

item.title: Oil Markets Will Take Time to Fix After US-Iran Ceasefire
item.description: The world’s shipowners are awaiting clarity on transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Top UK Port Operator’s £10 Billion Sale Draws KKR, GIP, DP World
item.description: The sale of the controlling stake in Associated British Ports has attracted initial interest from potential bidders including KKR & Co. as well as BlackRock Inc.’s Global Infrastructure Partners, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: Chevron, Delta, Exxon, Levi Strauss, Meta
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures soar 2.8% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York after the US and Iran reached a temporary ceasefire deal which will allow ships to sail through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Russia Boosts Oil Income to Highest Since Early in Ukraine War
item.description: Soaring prices, recovery in flows lift value of exports to most since June 2022

item.title: Booming Hedge-Fund Options Trade Suffers Worst Month in Decade
item.description: A trade that went from niche to one of the biggest options strategies on Wall Street in recent years posted its worst monthly performance in more than a decade in March, underscoring how the shock of the war in Iran unraveled popular investment approaches.

item.title: SoftBank Unit Plans Debut Euro Bond as Group Makes Big AI Push
item.description: SoftBank Corp., a unit of Japanese conglomerate SoftBank Group Corp., is preparing to sell its first euro-denominated bond as founder Masayoshi Son makes a big push into artificial intelligence.

item.title: StanChart Weighs Partial Takeover of Crypto Custodian Zodia
item.description: Standard Chartered Plc is seeking to fully merge parts of majority-owned Zodia Custody Ltd. with one of its digital-asset operations, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: Aerospace, Defense Parts Maker Arxis Seeks $1.06 Billion in IPO
item.description: Arxis Inc., a maker of electronic and mechanical parts for aerospace and defense firms is seeking to raise as much as $1.06 billion in its US initial public offering.

item.title: High Dividends Turn Telecom Stocks Into Market’s New Haven Play
item.description: An uncertain economy and dangerous geopolitical backdrop have investors buying into technology’s most popular haven trade: dividend-rich telecommunications stocks.

item.title: Social Media Companies Are Looking for a New Name
item.description: Meta and YouTube try to redefine themselves in light of increasing criticism

item.title: How European Winemakers, Importers Absorbed Trump’s Tariff Punch
item.description: Stockpiling, bonded warehouses and thinner margins helped wine dealers offset higher US import taxes

item.title: Nations Are Warned Against Worsening Fiscal Gaps With New Aid
item.description: Confronted by a stagflationary hit thanks to the Iran war, governments are weighing fiscal responses.

item.title: Kenya Reaches French Defense Deal as West Africa Ends Army Ties
item.description: Kenyan lawmakers are expected to ratify a defense pact with France on Wednesday, boosting relations at a time when former colonies in West Africa have been terminating long-standing military ties.

item.title: Swatch Urges Shareholders to Reject US Activist’s Board Election
item.description: Swatch Group AG recommended its shareholders vote against US activist investor Steven Wood’s bid to join the Swiss watchmaker’s board.

item.title: Qatar Postpones Sixth Edition of Flagship Annual Finance Forum
item.description: Doha’s flagship annual Qatar Economic Forum conference will be delayed until later this year amid widespread disruptions to regional events caused by the Iran war.

item.title: Ex-Rio Tinto Boss to List Seabed Mining Firm in $1 Billion Deal
item.description: American Ocean Minerals Corp., a deep-sea exploration company led by former Rio Tinto Chief Executive Officer Tom Albanese, is combining with Odyssey Marine Exploration Inc. in a reverse merger, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: German Dentists’ Fund Sues Its Ex-Manager for €50 Million
item.description: A German dentist pension fund that’s facing a loss of half its assets sued its ex-managing director for about €50 million ($58 million).

item.title: Relief Sweeps Markets After Two-Week Ceasefire Deal
item.description: Oil plunges and traders revive bets on interest rate cuts from the Fed.

item.title: India Buys First Iran Oil Cargo Since 2019 as War Curbs Supply
item.description: State-owned refiner Indian Oil Corp. has purchased a cargo of Iranian oil which would be the country’s first such delivery since 2019, according to people familiar with the matter, as weeks of war in the Persian Gulf upend the energy trade.

item.title: Finland Is Working Toward IPO of Gambling Company Veikkaus
item.description: Finland is taking step toward potentially listing state-owned gambling company Veikkaus Oy with several milestones still to be met before it’s ready for public markets.

item.title: Amundi Scooped Up Stocks When Most Investors Rushed for the Exit
item.description: Amundi SA has been buying into the selloff in equity markets just as most investors were slashing their stock exposure on fears of an escalation in the Iran war. It paid off.

item.title: South Africa Leads Rebound in Emerging Markets Hit By Iran Conflict
item.description: South Africa’s rand soared, government bond yields tumbled and stocks jumped the most in six years as investors piled back into emerging-market assets that were worst-hit by the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Argentines Plunge Into Debt for Bills, Cars and Birthday Parties
item.description: Cristian Dezilio thought the loan he took out to buy his son a used car to get around Buenos Aires’ suburbs was plenty manageable at $355 a month in pesos.

item.title: Samsung Holder Seeks to Sell Up to $2.1 Billion in Block Trade
item.description: A Samsung Electronics Co. shareholder is seeking to sell all their remaining shares in the company for up to 3.1 trillion won ($2.1 billion) through a block trade, according to terms of the deal seen by Bloomberg News, in one of the biggest such offerings in South Korea.

item.title: China Trumpets ‘Own Efforts’ in Pushing For US-Iran Ceasefire
item.description: A Chinese diplomat said Beijing had made its “own efforts” in pushing for a ceasefire between the US and Iran, shortly after Donald Trump credited China with playing a pivotal role in that deal.

item.title: White House Economists Say Stablecoin Rewards Won’t Harm Banks
item.description: Banning crypto firms from offering customers yield on stablecoins would not have a meaningful effect on community banks, White House economists said in a report on Wednesday — marking the latest development in a fierce conflict between the two industries that has stalled legislation in Congress.
